Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

UCP 9-9, 398-399 [Enuma Anu Enlil 33][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
oo NaN  (start of obverse missing)
o 1'o 1'

* U₄ ⸢17⸣-[KAM ...]

(o 1') (If there is a solar eclipse) on the 17th day: [...].

o 2'2'

* U₄ 18-KAM ⸢U₄⸣ [...]

(o 2') (If) on the 18th day (or) the [nth] day: [...].

o 3'3'

* U₄ 19-KAM LUGAL ina MU BI UŠ₂ [...]

(o 3') (If) on the 19th day: a king will die in that year. [...].

o 4'4'

* U₄ 20-KAM LUGAL SU.BIR₄ki ina gišTUKUL ⸢ŠUB⸣[ut ...]

(o 4') (If) on the 20th day: the king of Subartu will die by a weapon. [...]

o 5'5'

* U₄ 28-KAM LUGAL ŠU₂ GIG-ma TIuṭ ki-mu-[šu₂ ...]

(o 5') (If) on the 28th day: the king of the world will become ill and recover. His family [...]. In that year there will be a rising of the enemy and the land will become convulsed and [...] into a fortress.

o 6'6'

ina MU BI ZI KUR₂ GAL₂-ma KUR ir-ru-ur-ma ana KISLAḪ₃ [...]

o 7'7'

* U₄ 30.LA₂.1-KAM BAL LUGAL URIki TIL dIŠKUR ŠE I₃.DUB RAiṣ ip-[pi-ra ...]

(o 7') (If) on the 29th day: the king of Akkad's reign will end. Adad will devastate the grain of the granaries. Conflict [...].

o 8'8'

* U₄ 30-KAM BAL LUGAL SU₃ ina KUR SU.GU₇ IGI ip-pi-⸢ra⸣ [...]

(o 8') (If) on the 30th day: the king's reign will be long. Hunger will appear in the land. Conflict [...].

o 9'9'

* TA U₄ 1-KAM EN U₄ 30-KAM KAN₅ AŠ.TE ina KUR [...]

(o 9') (If) it is eclipsed from the 1st day to the 30th day: a throne in the land [...].

o 10'10'

* u₄-um KAN₅ ddele-bat u dU₄.AL.TAR KI-šu₂ IGI-MEŠ ana LUGAL SILIM ana KUR SU.GU₇

(o 10') (If) on the day it is eclipsed Venus and Jupiter appear with it: peace for the king, hunger for the land.

o 11'11'

* ina la ŠID-MEŠ-šu₂ KAN₅ NIG₂.ḪA.LAM.MA kurSU.BIR₄ki TIL : NIG₂.ḪA.LAM.MA KUR SU₃ti TIL

(o 11') (If) it is eclipsed on its uncounted (day): annihilation will destroy Subartu (or): annihilation will destroy a distant land.

o 12'12'

* ina itiBARA₂ a-dir me-tu₄ ina KUR GAL₂ LUGAL UŠ₂ DINGIR-šu₂ UŠ₂ : ina KUR NIG₂.ḪUL IGI : ana EREN₂ ⸢ḪUL IGI⸣

(o 12') (If) it is eclipsed in Nisannu (I): there will be a dead man in the land. A king will die. His god will die. All evil will appear in the land (or): evil will appear to the army.

o 13'13'

* ina itiGU₄ U₄ 1-KAM 20 KAN₅ KUR ip-par-ra-ar EREN₂-ḪI.A ZIa : * U₄ 10.LA₂.1-KAM ub-[bu]-⸢ṭu₂⸣ ina KUR GAL₂-MEŠ1

(o 13') (If) the Sun is eclipsed in Ayyaru (II) on the 1st day: the land will disperse. Armies will arise. : (If) on the 9th day: there will be starvation in the land.

o 14'14'

* U₄ 11-KAM BUN₂ u a-ki-lu₄ ina KUR GAL₂-MEŠ EREN₂ KUR IB₂.TUR : SU.GU₇ [...]

(o 14') (If) on the 11th day: there will be ... and caterpillars in the land. The army of the land will shrink (or): hunger [...].

o 15'15'

* U₄ 13-KAM LUGAL ṭa-ri-du-tu₂ DUak : SU.GU₇ [...]

(o 15') (If) on the 13th day: the king will become a fugitive (or): hunger [...].

o 16'16'

* U₄ 14-KAM ŠEG₃-MEŠ ANe ILLU-MEŠ ina IDIM DU-MEŠni : ub-bu-ṭu₂ GAL₂-MEŠ * U₄ 15-⸢KAM LUGAL⸣ ELAMki UŠ₂2

(o 16') (If) on the 14th day: rains from heaven, floods from the underground water will come (or): there will be starvation. (If) on the 15th day: the king of Elam will die.

o 17'17'

* U₄ 16-KAM LUGAL ana LUGAL MUNUS.KUR₂ KINar₂ : LUGAL SU.BIR₄ki ina gišTUKUL ŠUBut

(o 17') (If) on the 16th day: king will send enmity to king (or): the king of Subartu will die by a weapon.

o 18'18'

* U₄ 18-KAM LUGAL KUR-su BAL-su-ma NIG₂.GAR GAR šu-ut-ta-a-tu₄ ina KUR GAL₂-MEŠ

(o 18') (If) on the 18th day: the king - his land will revolt and there will be blasphemy (or): there will be hunters' pitfalls in the land.

o 19'19'

[...] KUR ub-bal ina KUR NIG₂.⸢GU₇⸣-ša₂ GU₇ : * U₄ 21-KAM LUGAL ana LUGAL MUNUS.KUR₂ KINar₂

(o 19') [...] will bring [...] land. They will enjoy its consumption in the land. : (If) on the 21st day: king will send enmity to king.

o 20'20'

[... SU].GU₇

(o 20') [...] hunger.

(rest of obverse missing)
Reverse
rr NaN  (start of reverse missing)
r 1'r 1'

* ⸢U₄⸣ [...]

(r 1') (If) on the [nth] day: [...].

r 2'2'

* U₄ 30-⸢KAM IRI LUGAL⸣ u ⸢UN-MEŠ⸣-[šu₂ ...]

(r 2') (If) on the 30th day: city, king, and his people [...].

r 3'3'

* TA U₄ 1-KAM EN U₄ 30-KAM KAN₅ LUGAL URIki KI.MIN kurURIki ZAḪ₂

(r 3') (If) it is eclipsed from the 1st day to the 20th day: the king of the city of Akkad; alternatively, the land of Akkad will be destroyed.

r 4'4'

* u₄-um KAN₅ ddele-bat u dU₄.AL.TAR KI-šu₂ IGI-MEŠ IRI LUGAL u UN-MEŠ-šu₂ SILIM-MEŠ

(r 4') (If) on the day that it is eclipsed Venus and Jupiter appear with it: city, king, and his people will be at peace. Lions will go wild. The ways will be cut off.

r 5'5'

UR.A-ME IDIM-MEŠ A.RA₂ [KU₅]-MEŠ

r 6'6'

* ina la ŠID-MEŠ-šu₂ KAN₅ UŠ₂-MEŠ ina KUR GAL₂-MEŠ LUGAL KI LUGAL KUR₂ir ŠUBti₃ UN-MEŠ KI.[MIN EREN₂]ni KI.MIN LUGAL KUR₂ir

(r 6') (If) it is eclipsed on its uncounted (day): there will be deaths in the land. King will become hostile to king. Epidemic of the people; alternatively, the army. Alternatively, the king will become hostile.

r 7'7'

* ina itiKIN KAN₅ LUGAL UŠ₂-ma KUR ŠA₃.ḪUL IGI KI.MIN ŠA₃.⸢ḪUL⸣ [...]

(r 7') (If) it is eclipsed in Ululu (VI): a king will die and the land will experience sorrow. Alternatively, sorrow [...].

r 8'8'

ŠU.NIGIN 1 UŠ 44 MU-DIDLI DUB 33-KAM₂ EŠ₂.GAR₃ ša₂ * U₄ d[60 ...]

(r 8') Total: 1 sixty, 44 lines. Tablet 33 of the series of "When Anu [...]".

(catchline)
r 9'9'

* ina itiZIZ₂ U₄ 1-KAM 20 KAN₅ LUGAL ana LUGAL MUNUS.KUR₂ KIN⸢ar₂⸣

(r 9') (If) the Sun is eclipsed in Šabaṭu (XI) on the 1st day: king will send enmity to king.

(colophon)
r 10'10'

GIM SUMUN-šu₂ SAR-ma ba-ru₃ u up-puš₄ GABA.RIe gišDA SUMUNbar IM d60—ŠEŠ—GAL₂ši

(r 10') Written, checked and properly executed according to its original. Copy of an old writing board. Tablet of Anu-aha-ušabši, son of Kidin-Anu, descendant of Ekur-zakir, incantation priest of Anu and Antu. [...] Reš temple, scribe of "When Anu, Ellil".

r 11'11'

DUMU ša₂ mki-din—d60 lu₂ŠA₃.BAL.BAL mE₂.KUR—za-kir lu₂MAŠ.MAŠ ⸢d60 u an-tu₄⸣

r 12'12'

[... e₂]⸢re⸣-eš lu₂UMBISAG * U₄ d⸢60 d⸣[EN.LIL₂.LA₂ ...]

(rest of reverse missing)

1The plural marker on GAL₂ is unexpected here.

2the plural marker on GAL₂ is unexpected here
